name: university_donor_lab_naming_scandal
premise: Provost Danielle Mercer has learned that a billionaire alumnus whose name is about to go on the university's new biomedical research center privately paid a consulting firm to suppress workplace-harassment complaints at one of his companies. The naming ceremony is in six days, and the donor's final fifty million dollar installment will fund scholarships, lab equipment, and the debt service on the building itself. Student reporters have obtained part of the consulting report and plan to publish the story tomorrow. The board chair wants Danielle to proceed with the naming until the allegations are "better substantiated," while faculty senators argue that using the donor's name would permanently stain the university's credibility on sexual-misconduct policy. Canceling now could trigger a legal fight and leave the center partially unfunded.

agents.name: Danielle Mercer
agents.identity: I am the provost of a university that asks students to trust our values every day, not just our branding. I know how much good this building could do, but I also know institutions hollow themselves out by pretending ethics can wait until after the ribbon cutting.
agents.goal: I want to protect the university's financial stability without sacrificing its integrity in public view. I need to decide whether due process for the donor justifies moving ahead or whether the symbolic harm is already too great.

agents.name: Board Chair Leonard Hsu
agents.identity: I am the board chair responsible for keeping this institution solvent through a brutal funding climate. To me, an allegation in the middle of a capital campaign is not the same thing as a finding, and overreaction can damage students who had nothing to do with the donor.
agents.goal: I want Danielle to proceed with the naming while the university reviews the allegations, because losing the gift now could cripple research and student aid. I believe institutions should not abandon a commitment on the basis of incomplete reporting.

choices: I will suspend the naming ceremony and reopen the gift review, accepting financial and legal fallout in order to protect the university's moral credibility.
I will proceed with the naming for now while launching an internal review, accepting reputational risk in order to preserve the center's funding and commitments.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Danielle Mercer must decide whether to suspend the donor naming ceremony or proceed while reviewing the allegations. What does Danielle Mercer do?
